question: Are PPF contributions eligible under Section 80C?
ground_truth: Yes, contributions made to the Public Provident Fund (PPF) qualify for deduction under Section 80C, subject to the overall limit of ₹1,50,000. PPF also enjoys EEE (Exempt-Exempt-Exempt) tax status — contribution, interest, and maturity are all tax-free. This deduction is only available under the Old Tax Regime.
source: Income Tax Act, Section 80C; PPF Scheme Rules

question: How many days must an individual stay in India to qualify as a Resident for tax purposes?
ground_truth: Under Section 6(1), an individual is a Resident if: (a) present in India for 182 days or more during the previous year; or (b) present in India for 60 days or more during the previous year and 365 days or more in the preceding 4 years. For Indian citizens leaving for employment abroad, or citizens/PIOs visiting India, the 60-day condition is replaced by 182 days. From FY 2020-21, a citizen with income > ₹15 lakh and not taxable in any other country may be treated as deemed resident.
source: Income Tax Act, Section 6; Finance Act 2020

- 6 months for the first 6 months of the tax year
- 3 months for the next 6 months of the tax year
- 3 months for the last 3 months of the tax year

question: Can long-term capital loss be set off against short-term capital gains?
ground_truth: No. Under Section 74, long-term capital loss (LTCL) can only be set off against long-term capital gains (LTCG), not against short-term capital gains. However, short-term capital loss (STCL) can be set off against both short-term and long-term capital gains. Unabsorbed capital losses (both LTCL and STCL) can be carried forward for 8 assessment years.
source: Income Tax Act, Section 74; Section 70

question: What is the tax treatment of income from Virtual Digital Assets (VDAs) such as crypto?
ground_truth: Under Section 115BBH introduced in Finance Act 2022, income from transfer of Virtual Digital Assets (including cryptocurrency and NFTs) is taxed at 30% plus surcharge and 4% cess. No deductions are allowed except the cost of acquisition. Losses from VDA cannot be set off against any other income or carried forward. TDS at 1% under Section 194S applies to VDA transactions above the prescribed threshold (₹50,000 or ₹10,000 depending on the payer type).
source: Income Tax Act, Section 115BBH; Section 194S; Finance Act 2022

question: What is TCS on foreign remittance under the Liberalised Remittance Scheme (LRS)?
ground_truth: Under Section 206C(1G), Tax Collected at Source (TCS) applies on foreign remittances under LRS: 20% on all remittances exceeding ₹7 lakh per year (except education/medical remittances where the rate is 5%). question: When is a tax audit mandatory under Section 44AB?
ground_truth: Tax audit under Section 44AB is mandatory when: (a) Business turnover exceeds ₹1 crore (₹10 crore if 95%+ receipts and payments are digital); (b) Professional gross receipts exceed ₹50 lakh; (c) A person opts out of presumptive taxation (44AD/44ADA) or declares income below presumptive rates. The audit must be conducted by a Chartered Accountant and the report filed in Form 3CA/3CB and 3CD by 30th September of the assessment year.
source: Income Tax Act, Section 44AB; Finance Act 2021

question: Is linking PAN with Aadhaar mandatory and what are the consequences of non-linking?
ground_truth: Yes. Under Section 139AA, quoting Aadhaar in the income tax return and linking PAN with Aadhaar is mandatory for resident individuals eligible to obtain Aadhaar. Failure to link by 31 May 2024 renders PAN inoperative. An inoperative PAN results in: TDS/TCS at higher rates (Section 206AA/206CC), inability to open bank accounts, demat accounts, or carry out high-value transactions. A late linking fee of ₹1,000 is payable. PAN becomes operative again once linked.
source: Income Tax Act, Section 139AA; Finance Act 2017; CBDT Circular 3/2023

question: What is CPC in income tax and what is its role?
ground_truth: CPC stands for Centralized Processing Centre, located in Bengaluru. It processes income tax returns filed electronically under Section 143(1) without any interface with the taxpayer. CPC issues intimations under Section 143(1) after processing — confirming the tax return as filed, or identifying discrepancies between reported income and data available (TDS mismatch, etc.). Refunds are also processed and credited by CPC. CPC is managed by the Income Tax Department and operated through TRACES/e-filing portal.
source: Income Tax Act, Section 143(1); Income Tax Department, CPC Bengaluru
